description: Coherence Phase 1, second PRD. Carve src/loop_engine/engine.rs (9644 lines) into orchestrator.rs / iteration.rs / wave_scheduler.rs / slot.rs / recovery.rs while preserving the five layers of parallel-slot cascade defenses, the overflow ladder, the auto-review trigger, the shared iteration_pipeline.rs parity contract, and the operator escape valve (check_override_invalidation). Strengthens the iteration_pipeline parity invariant into a compile-time / test-time assertion. Hard prerequisite: prd-tasklifecycle-extraction.md (prefix 035925a9) MUST have merged first so the LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint is green and the carve does not have to also move ~15 status-write sites. Boundary contract with the parallel runner-trait-hygiene Phase 2 effort: whichever PRD merges first leaves clear seams.

criteria: Rust: `cargo check --all-targets --all-features` passes with no new warnings
Rust: Scoped tests for touched files pass with `cargo test`
No new .unwrap() / .expect() in production paths (existing ones move verbatim; #[cfg(test)] unchanged)
No new raw `UPDATE tasks SET status` SQL anywhere — LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint stays green
Static-dispatch RunnerKind path preserved on every spawn boundary (no Box<dyn LlmRunner>)
Send + Sync invariants on SlotPromptBundle + LlmRunner preserved (compile-time assertion in prompt/mod.rs still passes)
Public surface stable: `cargo check --all-targets` passes without import changes outside src/loop_engine/
Comments explain WHY (boundary contracts, defense-layer invariants), never narrate WHAT or the extraction itself
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks in this PRD

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after REVIEW-001 passes the full quality gate AND the dogfood gate (10 iterations across 2 distinct PRDs with byte-identical observable behavior). Squash optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when carving the loop engine
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate which extraction order minimizes blast radius (bottom-up: recovery → slot → wave_scheduler → iteration → orchestrator)

2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Extract recovery.rs even though Approach B (keep in engine.rs) would be cheaper now; future recovery primitives compound. ~1 day extra now saves ~2+ weeks of next-refactor churn (1:10+ ratio)
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Byte-identical observable behavior is non-negotiable. Same stderr, same DB state, same exit codes
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically; all five defense-layer regression tests stay green
5. CODE QUALITY — qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings, visibility ladder respected (pub(super) → pub(crate) → pub)
6. POLISH — Module rustdocs, CLAUDE.md touchpoints table, retrospective appendix
principles: This is a refactor, not a hardening pass. Existing .unwrap()s, error-handling shapes, and stderr emissions move verbatim.
Carve along existing seams, do not invent new ones. The PRD names the target modules and the symbols each owns; the implementer does not redesign.
Each extraction is one commit (or one PR) — never split a function's body across commits.
Defense layers move with regression tests in scope; if a defense layer's test moves between files, the test moves in the same commit as the code it covers.
Dogfood gate is the final arbiter — pre-refactor stderr/DB baseline vs. post-refactor must match byte-identically on the same input.
Boundary contract with runner-trait-hygiene Phase 2: avoid editing the same engine.rs hunks; coordinate via comments + reviewer overlap

prohibitedOutcomes: Adding a raw UPDATE tasks SET status site, even temporarily, anywhere in the new modules — LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint MUST stay green at every commit
Introducing dynamic dispatch (Box<dyn LlmRunner>, Box<dyn Scheduler>, etc.) on the hot path; static-dispatch RunnerKind enum stays the path
Recomputing slot 0's worktree path via compute_slot_worktree_path(_, branch, 0) — defense layer #1 hinges on threading the path from ensure_slot_worktrees
Splitting the apply_pending_promotion / escalate_task_model_if_needed_inner pair across commits — the after-tx.commit() invariant must move atomically
Removing or refactoring the SYNTHETIC_DEADLOCK_SLOT sentinel — defense layer #4's deadlock-guard contract depends on it
Widening visibility beyond what an extraction strictly requires (pub(super) → pub(crate) → pub ladder)
Adding pub use chains that pierce three or more modules to preserve a single import path
Renaming exported symbols (run_loop, run_iteration, run_wave_iteration, process_slot_result, etc.) — public surface stability per FR-008
Editing iteration_pipeline.rs's production code (the FR-006 assertion lives in tests/ or as a #[cfg(test)] block)
Skipping the dogfood gate. 10 iterations × 2 distinct PRDs with byte-identical stderr/DB capture is REVIEW-001's gate
Tidying or refactoring extracted code during the move. Carve first, polish (if needed) in a separate REFACTOR-xxx after REFACTOR-REVIEW-FINAL

requires.reason: TaskLifecycle service must own every status write (LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint green) before the carve begins — otherwise the carve would also have to move the ~15 raw UPDATE sites in engine.rs, which inflates blast radius and creates merge hell against an in-flight migration.

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: task-mgr show 035925a9-MILESTONE-FINAL shows status=done
git log on main shows the merge commit for the tasklifecycle-extraction branch
grep test for raw `UPDATE tasks SET status` outside src/lifecycle/ returns only LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION-annotated sites (init/mod.rs bootstrap)
src/loop_engine/engine.rs claim_slot_task calls TaskLifecycle::try_claim (not raw SQL)
If any check fails, emit <promise>BLOCKED</promise> with a clear reason
userStories.description: Verify prd-tasklifecycle-extraction.md (prefix 035925a9) has reached MILESTONE-FINAL and been merged to main. Confirm the LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint test exists and is green. Confirm claim_slot_task routes through TaskLifecycle::try_claim. Output <promise>BLOCKED</promise> with task ID and reason if not. The Cluster A serialization rule (per coherence design — Dogfood concurrency, N-iteration exit gate) forbids parallel PRDs in this cluster.
userStories.notes: This is the Cluster A serialization gate from the coherence design. Do not start any extraction work until this task passes.
qualityDimensions: Fail-closed: any check ambiguity is a BLOCKED, not a proceed
The check is a one-time gate; subsequent iterations do not re-check (they read the green state from CLARIFY-001's progress note)
userStories.title: Gate: TaskLifecycle Extraction PRD must merge first

acceptanceCriteria: Two baseline scenarios chosen and named (one sequential, one 2-slot wave) and their input PRD + initial git state recorded
Baseline stderr captures saved (one per scenario) with timestamps stripped or normalized to a sentinel so REVIEW-001's diff is signal-only

Baseline DB-final-state snapshot saved via a single deterministic command captured verbatim in the progress log (proposal: `sqlite3 .task-mgr/tasks.db .dump | sed -E 's/[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[^"]+/TIMESTAMP/g; s/run_[0-9a-f]{8,}/run_ID/g' | sort > tests/fixtures/refactor-baselines/<scenario>.db.txt`)

Baselines checked into git under tests/fixtures/refactor-baselines/ with a one-line README.md noting: 'Frozen pre-refactor captures of engine.rs behavior; regenerating requires checking out main at <commit-sha> and re-running the capture commands recorded in the ANALYSIS-001 progress section.' This makes the dogfood gate reviewable and machine-portable.

Baseline reproducibility check: capture twice on main (with the normalizer applied each time), `diff` the two captures, confirm zero output. If non-zero, the chosen scenario is non-deterministic and must be replaced or constrained

Wave-mode stderr ordering: document explicitly whether today's stderr is deterministic line-by-line (slot output is captured to per-slot files and serialized in slot-index order) OR whether the contract is set-equality after normalization. If non-deterministic, REVIEW-001's diff uses sorted-line comparison; otherwise byte-identical
RunnerKind match site inventory in progress log: file:line, surrounding context, KIND-CORRECT or CAPABILITY-MISLABELED label, one-line rationale
Existing inventory from grep `RunnerKind::Grok\|RunnerKind::Claude` src/loop_engine/engine.rs starts the count at ~15 sites; cover every src/ hit
userStories.description: Two outputs: (1) Capture a pre-refactor stderr + DB-final-state baseline for the dogfood gate. Run two representative loop scenarios (one sequential PRD, one 2-slot wave PRD) against current main, save the stderr captures and a DB-final-state snapshot to a private path (e.g., .task-mgr/refactor-baselines/). REVIEW-001 will diff against these. (2) Inventory every `RunnerKind::Claude` / `RunnerKind::Grok` match in src/ with line numbers and a one-line classification (KIND-CORRECT or CAPABILITY-MISLABELED) — this is shared work with the parallel runner-trait-hygiene Phase 2 PRD's FR-005 audit; the inventory lives in progress log for both PRDs to consume.
userStories.notes: Pick PRDs that exercise the relevant defenses: sequential = curate-session-cleanup.json (mostly sequential, exercises overflow ladder); wave = parallel-task-execution.json (exercises slot-0 path threading, halt threshold, ephemeral overlay). If those PRDs are unavailable, pick equivalents. The RunnerKind audit is shared with prd-01-runner-capability-enforcement.md FR-005; either PRD's owner can produce it first; the other consumes it.
qualityDimensions: Baselines are deterministic — re-running on main produces a byte-identical capture (modulo intentionally-normalized timestamps / run-ids)
RunnerKind audit annotations are honest — if classification is ambiguous, the annotation says so; reviewers and the parallel runner-PRD author rely on this being signal, not opinion
Baselines live under tests/fixtures/refactor-baselines/ (checked into git so the dogfood gate is reproducible across machines and reviewable as a frozen artifact)
userStories.title: Pre-refactor baseline capture + RunnerKind audit

acceptanceCriteria: Test exists in tests/iteration_pipeline_parity.rs (or as #[cfg(test)] inside iteration_pipeline.rs)

Test references both call sites by their post-carve location (iteration.rs and slot.rs) — initially this is engine.rs:run_iteration and engine.rs:process_slot_result; FEAT tasks update the references as they extract

Test mechanism: each call site has a #[cfg(test)] helper fn (e.g., call_sites_destructure_full_params(params: ProcessingParams<'_>)) that exhaustively destructures `params` without using `..`. The test calls both helpers.

Known-bad discriminator: a test that deliberately adds a field to ProcessingParams without updating both helpers MUST fail to compile (verify manually during implementation by tentatively adding a field, observing compile error, removing it)
Test passes on current main (it codifies an existing invariant, not a new one)
userStories.description: Add a test or compile-time assertion that BOTH `iteration::run_iteration` (sequential) and `slot::process_slot_result` (wave) call `iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output` with a `ProcessingParams` built from exhaustively-destructured fields. Adding a new field to `ProcessingParams` without updating both call sites must fail to compile. Today this invariant is maintained by a comment in `prompt/mod.rs`; this test mechanically enforces it. Lives in tests/iteration_pipeline_parity.rs OR as a #[cfg(test)] block inside iteration_pipeline.rs.
edgeCases: ProcessingParams currently has N fields — the test destructure enumerates all N exhaustively, never using `..`

A future field added to ProcessingParams that is only meaningful to one path (e.g., wave-only telemetry) must still appear in both destructures — the field's presence in `params` is what enforces parity; conditional consumption inside the path is allowed but the field reference is mandatory

failureModes.cause: Helper uses `..` (rest pattern) for ergonomic destructure
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Code review rejects; the explicit destructure is the entire enforcement mechanism

invariants: Adding a field to ProcessingParams without updating both helpers fails to compile
Removing a field from ProcessingParams without updating both helpers fails to compile
Renaming a field in ProcessingParams without updating both helpers fails to compile
userStories.notes: Phase 2 foundation: this assertion is the long-term enforcement mechanism. The PRD's biggest risk (wave/sequential divergence as fields drift) is closed by this test. Spend the time to make it robust — manual verification that adding a field breaks compile is the acceptance test for the test itself.
qualityDimensions: The test must actually fail to compile (not run-time fail) when ProcessingParams drifts — the whole point is compile-time enforcement
The test is structural — it does not assert behavior, only that both call sites destructure exhaustively
Does not touch iteration_pipeline.rs's production code beyond adding the #[cfg(test)] helpers if that location is chosen
userStories.title: iteration_pipeline parity assertion test (FR-006)

acceptanceCriteria: Inventory in progress log: defense layer 1-5, test file:test_fn for each, currently passing on main (cargo test confirms)

For each defense, the symbols it tests (e.g., `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver`, `apply_merge_fail_reset_and_halt_check`, `reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots`, `select_parallel_group`, `classify_ephemeral_branch`) are listed

Any defense layer that LACKS a dedicated regression test today is flagged as a gap — gaps spawn an IMPL-FIX or a separate TEST-xxx task before the corresponding FEAT extraction touches the defense's symbols
Slot-0 safety guard (slot==0 rejection in classify_ephemeral_branch) has at least one negative-case test confirmed
userStories.description: Inventory every existing test that proves one of the five parallel-slot defenses (slot-path threading, halt threshold, implicit-overlap baseline, ephemeral overlay, stale ephemeral hygiene). For each, confirm it (a) exists and is currently passing on main, (b) will continue to compile/pass after the extraction (i.e., the defense's symbols are not renamed by this refactor). Record the inventory in the progress log so REVIEW-001 can audit at the end.
edgeCases: Stale `<branch>-slot-0` ref in test fixture: classify_ephemeral_branch returns Err
SYNTHETIC_DEADLOCK_SLOT path: handle_ephemeral_deadlock with malformed-suffix-only blockers populates failed_merges with usize::MAX entry
Empty touchesFiles for buildy-prefix task: claims __shared_infra__ slot anyway (selection.rs heuristic)
halt_threshold=0: legacy log-and-continue behavior preserved bit-for-bit on the same forced-fail input
Cross-wave ephemeral overlay deadlock: ephemeral_block_diagnostics populated AND trips FEAT-002 reset/halt

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Spawn a TEST-xxx task to add one BEFORE the corresponding FEAT extraction begins; the FEAT depends on the new test

invariants: Each defense layer has at least one regression test; the refactor cannot ship if a defense has no test
All defense tests currently pass on main — the inventory baseline
userStories.notes: See src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md `## Parallel-slot scheduling` section for the canonical defense-layer list (1-5). Touch the test files only to verify their existence and current pass status; do not modify them in this task. Any gap-fill TEST-xxx spawned here is its own task.
qualityDimensions: Inventory is exhaustive — if you cannot find a test for defense layer N, flag the gap explicitly rather than silently moving on
Inventory is honest — a test that asserts 'no crash' or that exercises an unrelated symbol does not count as covering the defense
Inventory is grep-verified — file:line citations match what's actually in main
userStories.title: Defense-layer regression test inventory

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/slot.rs created with the listed symbols
src/loop_engine/engine.rs no longer defines those symbols
src/loop_engine/mod.rs declares `pub(crate) mod slot;` and re-exports any items consumed from `crate::loop_engine::` paths (e.g., `pub use slot::run_slot_iteration` if external callers exist)
SlotContext, SlotResult, SlotEarlyExit, SlotFailureKind types stay in engine.rs; slot.rs imports them
claim_slot_task still routes through TaskLifecycle::try_claim (no raw SQL — LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint stays green)

process_slot_result still calls iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output (parity assertion from TEST-INIT-001 still passes; the test's call-site reference updates to `slot::process_slot_result` in this commit)
Existing wave-mode tests in tests/parallel_* continue to pass without modification

Defense layer #5 slot-0 safety guard preserved: classify_ephemeral_branch still returns Err for slot=0 (this lives in worktree.rs and is not touched by this extraction; verified by re-running the existing test)
cargo test -p task-mgr passes after the extraction
engine.rs line count decreased by ~700 lines (combined slot symbols)
userStories.description: Move slot-related symbols from engine.rs into src/loop_engine/slot.rs. Symbols: run_slot_iteration (engine.rs:557), slot_early_exit (engine.rs:512), claim_slot_task (engine.rs:787), slot_failure_result (engine.rs:818), process_slot_result (engine.rs:1409). The SlotContext / SlotResult / SlotEarlyExit / SlotFailureKind types stay in engine.rs and are re-exported (consumed by IterationContext + tests outside the loop_engine module). slot.rs has no cross-module dependency yet (recovery.rs and other modules still in engine.rs); it imports recovery functions from their engine.rs location and those imports update in FEAT-002 when recovery.rs lands. process_slot_result still calls iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output — that contract is preserved.
userStories.notes: First extraction. slot.rs imports recovery functions from their current engine.rs locations; those `use crate::loop_engine::engine::{check_override_invalidation, ...}` lines update to `use crate::loop_engine::recovery::{...}` in FEAT-002 when recovery.rs lands. Bottom-up extraction order: slot.rs is the leaf consumer that wave_scheduler.rs (FEAT-003) will import; recovery.rs (FEAT-002) is the cross-cutting helper. Architect-rec order: slot before recovery so the second extraction (recovery) exercises the cross-module call shape with a real consumer (slot.rs).
qualityDimensions: Byte-identical observable behavior on a 2-slot wave-mode dogfood scenario
claim_slot_task's WHERE id = ? AND status IN ('todo', 'in_progress') predicate semantics preserved exactly via TaskLifecycle::try_claim — no behavior change
Send + Sync on SlotPromptBundle preserved (compile-time assertion in prompt/mod.rs still passes)

Thread-spawn boundary in run_parallel_wave (slot iterations run on separate threads) preserved exactly — slot.rs::run_slot_iteration retains its full signature including any Send + Sync bounds on inputs
The TEST-INIT-001 parity assertion's call-site reference updates atomically in this commit (no transient broken state where the assertion points at the old engine.rs::process_slot_result)
userStories.title: Extract slot.rs — slot lifecycle + result processing (first extraction)

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/recovery.rs created with all listed symbols
src/loop_engine/engine.rs no longer defines those symbols
src/loop_engine/slot.rs imports updated: `use crate::loop_engine::recovery::{...}` (replacing the temporary `use crate::loop_engine::engine::{...}` from FEAT-001) — atomic with this commit
src/loop_engine/mod.rs declares `pub(crate) mod recovery;`
Public re-exports preserved: `crate::loop_engine::{escalate_task_model_if_needed, should_auto_block, ...}` if they were public, via `pub use recovery::...` in mod.rs
Existing tests for the recovery cluster (transactional promotion, override-invalidation, auto-block) move with their code in the same commit
apply_pending_promotion is called ONLY after tx.commit()? returns Ok — the inner/apply pattern moves atomically (one commit)
PendingPromotion type moves with the helpers (same file)
cargo test -p task-mgr passes after the extraction
LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint stays green
engine.rs line count decreased by approximately the size of the extracted cluster (~1000 lines)
userStories.description: Move the per-task recovery cluster from engine.rs into a new src/loop_engine/recovery.rs. Symbols: check_crash_escalation, check_override_invalidation, should_auto_block, should_escalate_for_consecutive_failures, apply_pending_promotion, escalate_task_model_if_needed_inner, escalate_task_model_if_needed, increment_consecutive_failures, reset_consecutive_failures, auto_block_task, handle_task_failure, prompt_overflow_result, probe_rate_limit_lifted, update_trackers, normalize_baseline, PendingPromotion type. After this extraction, slot.rs (already extracted in FEAT-001) updates its imports from `crate::loop_engine::engine::{...}` to `crate::loop_engine::recovery::{...}` in the same commit. mod.rs adds `pub(crate) mod recovery;`. Existing tests in engine.rs covering the recovery cluster (transactional promotion pattern, override-invalidation escape valve, auto-block threshold) move with the cluster.
userStories.notes: Approach A per PRD §6. Approach B (leave recovery in engine.rs) was rejected because future recovery primitives compound the refactor cost. Tests for this cluster live in engine.rs today — they move in the SAME commit as the code, no exceptions. The slot.rs import-update is included in this task's commit because slot.rs is the first cross-module consumer of recovery functions; iteration.rs and wave_scheduler.rs come in later FEATs and will import recovery directly from the new location.
qualityDimensions: Byte-identical observable behavior on the dogfood-baseline scenarios — REVIEW-001's diff will catch any drift

Visibility ladder: items consumed only within recovery.rs are `pub(super)` or private; items consumed by iteration.rs/wave_scheduler.rs/slot.rs are `pub(crate)`; only items re-exported from `crate::loop_engine::` are `pub`
The transactional invariant (inner returns PendingPromotion → caller applies after tx.commit()?) is preserved verbatim. Do not 'simplify' the pattern.
WORKAROUND() markers (if any cross the boundary) move with their code

The check_override_invalidation order-before-resolve constraint (top of every iteration, before resolve_effective_runner) is preserved at the call site — but that call site is in iteration.rs/wave_scheduler.rs/slot.rs, not recovery.rs; recovery.rs just provides the function
slot.rs's import-update is atomic with this commit — no transient state where slot.rs imports a recovery symbol from engine.rs while recovery.rs already exists
userStories.title: Extract recovery.rs — per-task recovery cluster (Approach A)

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/wave_scheduler.rs created with the listed symbols
src/loop_engine/engine.rs no longer defines those symbols
src/loop_engine/mod.rs declares `pub(crate) mod wave_scheduler;` and re-exports run_wave_iteration if external callers exist
SYNTHETIC_DEADLOCK_SLOT = usize::MAX moves to wave_scheduler.rs verbatim (defense layer #4)

Slot-path-threading invariant (defense layer #1): wave_scheduler MUST pass slot_paths: &[PathBuf] from ensure_slot_worktrees through to merge_slot_branches_with_resolver — a unit test asserts the threading at type level
handle_ephemeral_deadlock still emits the synthetic-slot FailedMerge record (defense layer #4's deadlock-guard contract)
apply_merge_fail_reset_and_halt_check still trips the FEAT-002 consecutive-merge-fail halt at the configured threshold
Existing tests in src/loop_engine/worktree.rs and src/commands/next/selection.rs covering defense layers 1, 2, 3, 4 continue to pass without modification
Wave-mode dogfood scenario (2-slot, then 4-slot) completes with byte-identical DB state + stderr capture vs. pre-refactor baseline
cargo test -p task-mgr passes after the extraction
engine.rs line count decreased by ~1800 lines
userStories.description: Move wave-scheduling symbols from engine.rs into src/loop_engine/wave_scheduler.rs. Symbols: run_wave_iteration (engine.rs:1835), run_parallel_wave (engine.rs:871), wave_preflight_check (engine.rs:1093), handle_no_eligible_tasks (engine.rs:1158), handle_ephemeral_deadlock (engine.rs:1220), build_slot_contexts (engine.rs:1316), build_shared_slot_params (engine.rs:1358), build_slot_prompt_params (engine.rs:1377), count_remaining_active_tasks (engine.rs:1597), wait_inter_wave_delay (engine.rs:1615), reset_task_to_todo (engine.rs:1643), apply_merge_fail_reset_and_halt_check (engine.rs:1691), read_prd_implicit_overlap_files (engine.rs:1762), apply_post_merge_reconcile (engine.rs:1790), SYNTHETIC_DEADLOCK_SLOT constant. wave_scheduler.rs depends on slot.rs (FEAT-001) and recovery.rs (FEAT-002).
userStories.notes: Largest extraction by line count (~1800). Approach: move symbols in dependency-order within wave_scheduler.rs (build_* helpers first, then handle_*, then the orchestration entry points). Each step is a separate cargo check; do not let the half-extracted state survive across iterations. The test for defense layer #1 (slot-path threading) is NEW in this extraction — it's a regression test the existing code lacks; add it as the first thing inside wave_scheduler.rs.
qualityDimensions: All five defense-layer regression tests stay green throughout (run scoped suite after every step of the extraction)

The slot-path-threading defense (layer #1) gets a dedicated type-level test in wave_scheduler.rs: construct a WaveIterationParams and confirm slot_worktree_paths is the literal vector ensure_slot_worktrees returned (not a recomputation)
SYNTHETIC_DEADLOCK_SLOT sentinel moves together with handle_ephemeral_deadlock — the synthesis pattern emit-at-least-one-record contract is preserved

Run-level config caching (ProjectConfig + PRD-side implicit_overlap_files loaded ONCE at run_loop startup, threaded through WaveIterationParams) is preserved — wave_scheduler reads from cached references, never calls read_project_config or read_prd_implicit_overlap_files mid-wave
The PRD merge-fail halt threshold semantics preserved: 0=never halt, 1=halt on any failure, 2=halt after two consecutive (default)

userStories.description: Move src/loop_engine/engine.rs::run_iteration (engine.rs:2230) and any sequential-only helpers into src/loop_engine/iteration.rs. iteration.rs depends on recovery.rs (FEAT-002) for the recovery primitives it calls. run_iteration still calls iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output (parity preserved).
userStories.notes: Sequential iteration is the simpler of the two execution paths. Symbol set is smaller than wave_scheduler. The biggest risk is the order-before-resolve constraint for check_override_invalidation — read the existing code carefully before moving.
qualityDimensions: Byte-identical observable behavior on the sequential dogfood scenario

The check_override_invalidation order-before-resolve constraint is preserved by keeping the call in iteration.rs at the same point in the iteration body — verified by reading the diff and confirming the call sits before resolve_effective_runner
iteration.rs is < 2000 lines after extraction (PRD target ~1500); if it exceeds, identify which symbol or block should have moved elsewhere and document the deviation
The TEST-INIT-001 parity assertion's call-site reference updates atomically with this commit
No new dependency cycles introduced (iteration.rs → recovery.rs, never the other way)

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/orchestrator.rs created with the listed symbols
src/loop_engine/engine.rs no longer defines those symbols
src/loop_engine/mod.rs declares `pub(crate) mod orchestrator;` and `pub use orchestrator::run_loop;` (run_loop is the canonical public entry point)
LoopRunConfig and LoopResult stay in engine.rs as public types (consumed by external callers like main.rs)
Signal handler ownership: orchestrator owns the SignalFlag construction + arming; the flag is threaded through WaveIterationParams + SlotIterationParams + IterationContext exactly as before
Auto-review trigger: the existing auto_review::maybe_fire invocation in run_loop stays at the same point in the run lifecycle (after the loop body completes, before run_cmd::end)

Stale ephemeral reconcile (defense layer #5): the worktree::reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots call is preserved in orchestrator.rs and runs BEFORE ensure_slot_worktrees — order is contractual per CLAUDE.md Step 9.5
Both a sequential and a 2-slot wave dogfood scenario complete byte-identically

engine.rs line count is < 1500 (PRD success metric)
userStories.description: Move outer-loop symbols from engine.rs into src/loop_engine/orchestrator.rs. Symbols: run_loop (engine.rs:3048, ~1300 lines), setup_signal_handler (engine.rs:4562), on_run_completed (engine.rs:4609), record_session_guidance (engine.rs:4631), check_global_skills (engine.rs:2985), trigger_human_reviews (engine.rs:4426), query_human_review_tasks (engine.rs:4388), prompt_pending_key_decisions (engine.rs:4467). orchestrator.rs depends on iteration.rs (FEAT-004) and wave_scheduler.rs (FEAT-003) — it dispatches to one or the other at the iteration boundary.
userStories.notes: Largest single function by line count (run_loop ~1300 lines). This extraction is mostly a copy-paste because run_loop is already a self-contained outer body. The risk is the surrounding helpers (signal_handler, on_run_completed, etc.) — confirm each is referenced only from run_loop's body before assuming it's exclusively orchestrator territory. If a helper is called from iteration.rs or wave_scheduler.rs, it stays in engine.rs (or moves to recovery.rs).
qualityDimensions: Public surface stability: `crate::loop_engine::run_loop` remains importable from the same path (via `pub use orchestrator::run_loop` in mod.rs)
Stale ephemeral reconcile + auto-review trigger + decisions prompt all fire at the exact same points in the run lifecycle as before
Signal handler arming happens at the same point relative to the rest of run_loop's body — moving it earlier or later changes user-visible behavior on Ctrl-C
Batch-mode auto-review semantics preserved: ONE review at end-of-batch for the LAST successful PRD that met the threshold; never per-PRD
userStories.title: Extract orchestrator.rs — outer run_loop + lifecycle

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md Touchpoints table updated: every `src/loop_engine/engine.rs` reference is verified — either updated to the new module or confirmed to still be in engine.rs
Narrative section updates: any `engine.rs::<symbol>` mention is re-pointed (manual grep for `engine.rs::` and verify each)

Each new module file has a module-level rustdoc (//! at top of file) explaining: (a) what this module owns, (b) what it does NOT own (pointer to sibling modules), (c) the key invariants it preserves (defense layers, parity assertions, etc.)
orchestrator.rs rustdoc references iteration.rs and wave_scheduler.rs as the iteration dispatchers
iteration.rs rustdoc references iteration_pipeline.rs as the shared post-Claude pipeline AND recovery.rs as the per-task recovery cluster
wave_scheduler.rs rustdoc references slot.rs (per-slot work), iteration_pipeline.rs (shared), recovery.rs (per-task), and worktree.rs (merge-back)
slot.rs rustdoc references iteration_pipeline.rs (post-spawn processing) and recovery.rs (per-task recovery)
recovery.rs rustdoc explains the transactional invariant (inner returns PendingPromotion → caller applies after tx.commit()?)
cargo doc builds without warnings on the new modules
userStories.description: Update src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md so every Touchpoints row points at the new module file. Update narrative sections (Overflow recovery and diagnostics, Iteration pipeline (shared), Parallel-slot scheduling) where they reference engine.rs::<symbol> by name — re-point to the new location. Add a module-level rustdoc to each new module (orchestrator.rs, iteration.rs, wave_scheduler.rs, slot.rs, recovery.rs) explaining its scope, sibling modules, and the contracts it preserves. This is FR-007.
userStories.notes: This is the cleanup task after the carve. Run cargo doc to confirm rustdocs are valid. CLAUDE.md edits are the slowest part — there are ~40 Touchpoints rows and ~10 narrative references.
qualityDimensions: CLAUDE.md changes are line-level surgical, never wholesale rewrites — preserve the existing narrative's voice and ordering
Module rustdocs are concise (~10-30 lines each) and pointer-heavy, not narrative-heavy — readers navigate to sibling modules, they don't learn the system from one rustdoc
The 'Touchpoints' table is grep-verified: every `src/loop_engine/engine.rs` mention is either updated or explicitly justified as still-in-engine.rs
userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md touchpoints + add module rustdocs

acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 5 defense layers verified: regression test exists, references valid symbols, passes on the post-extraction branch
Each of the public surface items in PRD FR-008 verified: import path stable, no external callers had to update imports
Visibility audit: every `pub` in a new module is justified by an external consumer; otherwise downgraded to `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)`
No raw `UPDATE tasks SET status` introduced in any new module (LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint green)
No new dynamic dispatch on the hot path (grep for `Box<dyn LlmRunner>` or `Box<dyn Scheduler>` returns zero hits in src/loop_engine/)
Issues found, if any, become CODE-FIX-xxx / WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks added via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` with rootCause/exactFix/verifyCommand
If no issues found: progress note `## CODE-REVIEW-1 — no issues found, all defense layers preserved, public surface stable`
userStories.description: Review FEAT-001 through FEAT-006. For each defense layer (1-5), confirm the regression test still exists, still references valid symbols, and still passes. For each public surface item, confirm `crate::loop_engine::<symbol>` still resolves from the original import path. For each new module, confirm visibility is at the appropriate ladder rung (pub(super) → pub(crate) → pub). Audit for any silent behavior change. Spawn CODE-FIX-xxx / WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks for any issues. Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent for substantive review.
userStories.notes: Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent: `Agent(subagent_type='rust-python-code-reviewer', prompt='Review FEAT-001..006 of the engine orchestration carve. Focus: defense-layer preservation, public-surface stability, visibility ladder hygiene, no new dynamic dispatch, LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep stays green.')`. Spawn each finding as CODE-FIX-xxx / WIRE-FIX-xxx with task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001.
qualityDimensions: Review is reading + grepping, NOT implementing — fixes go into spawned CODE-FIX/WIRE-FIX tasks
Defense-layer audit is line-by-line against the TEST-INIT-002 inventory; any drift is a finding
Visibility audit catches both over-permissive (pub when pub(crate) suffices) and under-permissive (pub(super) when pub(crate) is needed) classifications
userStories.title: Code review: post-extraction quality + wiring + defense-layer preservation

acceptanceCriteria: Each new module surveyed for: near-duplicates, functions > 100 lines, excessive sibling imports, vague visibility
REFACTOR-xxx tasks spawned for substantive findings via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` (priority 71-85)
Small touchpoints fixed inline via /simplify rather than spawned (within this task's commit)
If no substantive issues: progress note `## REFACTOR-REVIEW-FINAL — no substantive refactors needed; the carve is clean`
Contract fidelity verified: each defense layer's invariant is asserted by a test (not just documented in CLAUDE.md)
userStories.description: Full-context review of the carve. Look for: (a) opportunities to consolidate near-duplicate code that ended up in two modules (e.g., similar slot-context build code in wave_scheduler.rs and orchestrator.rs), (b) complexity hotspots (functions > 100 lines that should be split now that the surrounding context has changed), (c) coupling hotspots (modules that import too many sibling-module items), (d) contract fidelity (every defense layer's invariants still expressed in code, not just CLAUDE.md). Spawn REFACTOR-xxx tasks for anything substantive. Use /simplify on small touchpoints.
userStories.notes: Final pass before REVIEW-001. If REFACTOR-xxx tasks are spawned, they must complete before REVIEW-001 can claim. Use `/simplify` on small touchpoints — don't file a task just to dedent a closure.
qualityDimensions: Findings are concrete (file:line + proposed change), not vague ('this could be cleaner')

The 'this is a refactor, not a hardening pass' constraint stays — REFACTOR-xxx tasks address structural issues introduced or revealed by the carve, NOT pre-existing tech debt unrelated to the extraction
userStories.title: Final refactor pass: DRY, complexity, coupling, contract fidelity

acceptanceCriteria: Full unscoped quality suite is GREEN: `cargo fmt --check && cargo check --all-targets --all-features && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test -p task-mgr` all exit 0

Dogfood gate sequential scenario: re-run the ANALYSIS-001 sequential baseline scenario against refactored code; stderr diff is empty (modulo intentionally-normalized timestamps / run-ids); DB-final-state snapshot diff is empty
Dogfood gate wave scenario: re-run the ANALYSIS-001 wave baseline scenario against refactored code; same byte-identical contract
Aggregate iteration count >= 10 across both scenarios (e.g., sequential PRD with 6 tasks + wave PRD with 5 tasks = 11 iterations, satisfies the gate)
src/loop_engine/engine.rs line count is < 1500 (`wc -l src/loop_engine/engine.rs`)
Every FR-008 public surface item is importable from its original path (verified by `cargo check` succeeding with the existing import statements in main.rs, lib.rs, tests/, etc.)
Every defense layer's regression test passes
iteration_pipeline parity assertion (TEST-INIT-001) passes
LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION grep lint stays green

If any pre-existing test failure exists in the full suite that predates this PRD: fix inline (≤12 failures) OR spawn a single FIX-xxx task via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001 and BLOCKED until resolved (> 12 unrelated failures only)
userStories.description: The PRD's exit criterion. Run: (1) Full unscoped quality gate (cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test — entire workspace). (2) Dogfood gate: 10 iterations across 2 distinct PRDs (one sequential, one 2-slot wave) on the refactored code; diff the stderr capture and DB-final-state snapshot against the ANALYSIS-001 baselines. Any divergence is a hard fail. (3) Verify engine.rs is < 1500 lines (PRD success metric). (4) Verify all FR-008 public surface items resolve from their original import paths. Fix every failure, including pre-existing failures in the full suite (per the milestone protocol; trunk-green is the invariant).
userStories.notes: This is the final milestone — equivalent to MILESTONE-FINAL in other PRDs. Update merge_strategy decision: per coherence design Cluster A, the dogfood gate is the rule before the next Cluster A PRD opens (there isn't one — Phase 1 is done at this point), but the parallel runner-trait-hygiene Phase 2 PRD may have its own milestone that this informs. Do NOT skip the dogfood gate even if CI is green; that gate exists precisely because CI alone doesn't catch byte-level behavior drift.
qualityDimensions: The dogfood diff is the source of truth. CI green + dogfood diff empty = refactor ships. CI green + dogfood diff non-empty = silent regression, fix before merge.
Pre-existing full-suite failures are this milestone's responsibility — the lean-path 'trunk-green is the invariant' rule applies. Punt only if the >12-unrelated-failures escape hatch fires.

The 10-iteration × 2-PRD count is a floor, not a target. If the scenarios chosen in ANALYSIS-001 produce 7 + 6 = 13 iterations, you've exceeded the gate; if they produce 4 + 3 = 7, pick longer scenarios or extend one PRD's task list.
userStories.title: Final gate: full quality suite + dogfood gate (10 iterations × 2 PRDs)
